title: symphyotrichum lateriflorum var. flagellare
intro: Symphyotrichum lateriflorum var. flagellare, also known as New England aster, is a perennial plant species native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to help soothe wounds and reduce fever. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as aiding in digestion and reducing anxiety, due to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. Herbal preparations of New England aster, such as teas and infusions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy in modern medicine.
